name: NIS2
description: Comprehensive paraphrased NIS2 cybersecurity self-assessment checklist for MVP use. This is not legal advice and not official legal text.

sections.title: Scope, governance and management accountability
sections.description: Governance, management responsibility, scope assessment and cybersecurity oversight.

requirements.question: Has the company assessed whether it is in scope of NIS2?
requirements.description: Check whether sector, size and activity criteria have been assessed and documented.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Has the company assessed whether it is in scope of NIS2?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 scope / Annex I-II.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Has management approved cybersecurity risk management measures?
requirements.description: Assess whether senior management has approved and oversees cybersecurity measures.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Has management approved cybersecurity risk management measures?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 20.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Are cybersecurity responsibilities clearly assigned?
requirements.description: Check whether roles and owners are defined for security governance and operations.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are cybersecurity responsibilities clearly assigned?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 20-21.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Does management receive cybersecurity status reporting?
requirements.description: Assess whether risk, incidents and improvement activities are reported to management.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Does management receive cybersecurity status reporting?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 20.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Are cybersecurity policies approved and maintained?
requirements.description: Check whether policies define security expectations and are reviewed periodically.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are cybersecurity policies approved and maintained?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21(2)(a).
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

sections.title: Risk management and asset overview
sections.description: Risk analysis, assets, dependencies and security planning.

requirements.question: Are critical assets, systems and services identified?
requirements.description: Verify that the company knows which systems support essential or important services.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are critical assets, systems and services identified?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Is a cybersecurity risk assessment performed regularly?
requirements.description: Assess whether threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood and impact are evaluated.

requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21(2)(a).

requirements.question: Are risk treatment measures documented and tracked?
requirements.description: Check whether risk owners, actions and deadlines are recorded.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are risk treatment measures documented and tracked?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21.
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.description: Assess whether an all-hazards approach is used, including physical environment.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are physical and environmental risks considered for critical systems?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21(2).
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Are dependencies on third parties and cloud services mapped?
requirements.description: Check whether critical supplier and service dependencies are documented.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are dependencies on third parties and cloud services mapped?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21(2)(d).
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

sections.title: Security controls and protection
sections.description: Technical and organisational controls for networks, systems, users and data.

requirements.question: Are access controls implemented for critical systems?
requirements.description: Check whether access is approved, role-based and regularly reviewed.

requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21(2)(i).
requirements.riskIfMissing: Missing this control increases compliance risk, reduces audit readiness, and may lead to security or regulatory issues.

requirements.question: Are logs collected and reviewed for critical systems?
requirements.description: Assess whether relevant security events are logged and monitored.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are logs collected and reviewed for critical systems?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.
requirements.exampleEvidence: Policy, procedure, screenshot, system export, contract, log, or documentation proving compliance with NIS2 Art. 21.

sections.title: Incident handling and reporting
sections.description: Incident detection, response, escalation and regulatory reporting readiness.

requirements.question: Is an incident response process documented?
requirements.description: Check whether incident roles, escalation paths and response steps are defined.

sections.title: Supply chain security

sections.title: Secure acquisition, development and maintenance
sections.description: Security in system acquisition, development, change management and vulnerability handling.

requirements.question: Are security requirements defined for new systems and services?
requirements.description: Check whether security requirements are included before procurement or development.
requirements.implementationGuide: Document and implement a control for: "Are security requirements defined for new systems and services?". Review current practice, assign an owner, define the process, and keep evidence updated for NIS2.

sections.title: Awareness, training and cyber hygiene
